steps.title: Identify the subject
steps.content: The subject is "Food writer J. Kenji López-Alt" — one person, singular. The relative clause "who explores the science behind cooking" is a nonrestrictive modifier; it does not change the subject's number.

steps.title: Rule: singular subject requires singular present-tense verb
steps.content:
Per-choice diagnosis:

A. "explains" — singular present-tense verb. López-Alt explains — subject and verb agree. Correct.

B. "explain" — plural present-tense verb (used with I/you/we/they); disagrees with singular López-Alt. Incorrect.

C. "have explained" — plural present-perfect verb; disagrees with singular subject and the passage uses simple present throughout. Incorrect.

D. "are explaining" — plural progressive verb; disagrees with singular subject. Incorrect.

A is correct.
